channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Cross River extends scrap metal ban by one month
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Cross River State extends its scrap metal ban by one month to curb vandalism and strengthen regulation. Learn more about the ongoing suspension and its imp
Read More: https://punchng.com/cross-river-extends-scrap-metal-ban-by-one-month/

item.title: NSCDC, Kogi monarchs partner to curb illegal coal mining
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The NSCDC and Ankpa Traditional Council in Kogi State are collaborating to combat illegal coal mining, promoting lawful operations and community developmen
Read More: https://punchng.com/nscdc-kogi-monarchs-partner-to-curb-illegal-coal-mining/

item.title: REAN urges action to accelerate Nigeria’s clean energy transition
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
REAN calls for concrete action to accelerate Nigeria’s clean energy transition, unlock investment, and expand energy access. Learn more about the push for
Read More: https://punchng.com/rean-urges-action-to-accelerate-nigerias-clean-energy-transition/

item.title: Do more to defeat terrorism, Tinubu urges N’East govs
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu charges North-East governors to intensify efforts against terrorism and banditry, highlighting economic stability and infrastructure commi
Read More: https://punchng.com/do-more-to-defeat-terrorism-tinubu-urges-neast-govs/

item.title: CAF dismisses reports of AFCON 2027 format change
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
CAF confirms the AFCON 2027 tournament will retain its 24-team format, dismissing reports of any changes. Qualifiers begin September 2026.
Read More: https://punchng.com/caf-dismisses-reports-of-afcon-2027-format-change/

item.title: Two die, two hospitalised after consuming drug in Ogun
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Two individuals have died and two hospitalised in Ogun State after allegedly consuming ‘laughing gas’. Police are investigating the suspected drug-related
Read More: https://punchng.com/two-die-two-hospitalised-after-consuming-drug-in-ogun/

item.title: LASUTH defends digitalisation over patient record concerns
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
LASUTH defends its ongoing digitalisation programme, assuring patients of progress despite concerns over manual record movement. Learn about the phased imp
Read More: https://punchng.com/lasuth-defends-digitalisation-over-patient-record-concerns/

item.title: US pastor convicted for stealing $340,000 from churches to gamble
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A US pastor has been convicted of stealing over $340,000 from two churches to fund gambling. Learn more about the conviction and upcoming sentencing.
Read More: https://punchng.com/us-pastor-convicted-for-stealing-340000-from-churches-to-gamble/

item.title: Trump praised Tinubu’s resolve, not anti-terrorism achievement, says APM
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
APM accuses Tinubu’s government of misleading Nigerians about Trump’s letter, stating Trump praised Tinubu’s resolve, not anti-terrorism achievements.
Read More: https://punchng.com/trump-praised-tinubus-resolve-not-anti-terrorism-achievement-says-apm/

item.title: More than 70% of recent home buyers were counting on mortgage rates to drop. Now they’re stuck.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Refinancing to a lower rate isn’t an option for many homeowners, who now face “financially unsustainable” mortgage payments.

item.title: ‎“Please Don’t Let Me Die” – Veteran Ngozi Nwosu Cries Out As She Battles Undisclosed Ailment, Seeks N30m
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nollywood veteran actress Ngozi Nwosu has cried out for help as she battles an unknown ailment. ‎The actress, via her Instagram page, shared a video of herself in the hospital, saying she has been battling the ailment for some time and needs to undergo three surgeries that will cost her N30 million.

item.title: Dangote Refinery raises $2.5bn through private equity placement
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Dangote Refinery on Thursday announced that it has successfully raised $2.5 billion in new equity through a private placement. The refinery disclosed this in a statement on Thursday. According to the firm, the $2.5 billion equity raise was 3.7 times oversubscribed relative to the initial offer size

item.title: FG Moves To Mediate In Ekiti, Kwara Border Dispute
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government has resolved to mediate in the lingering border dispute between Eda Oniyo community in Ekiti state and Obbo-Ayegunle In Kwara State. The Director General of National Boundary Commission (NBC), Surveyor Adamu Adaji, who presided over an Ekiti /Kwara joint meeting held to resol

item.title: 2027: Kaduna ADC Governorship Aspirant Mainan Zazzau Resigns From Party
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: As of the time of filing this report, Bello has not publicly explained the reasons behind his resignation or indicated his next political move. The post 2027: Kaduna ADC Governorship Aspirant Mainan Zazzau Resigns From Party appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Serie A: Modric signs one-year contract extension at Milan
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Luka Modric extends his stay at AC Milan for another year. The veteran midfielder expresses delight at continuing his journey with the Rossoneri.
Read More: https://punchng.com/serie-a-modric-signs-one-year-contract-extension-at-milan/

item.title: Brent oil prices rise above $100 first time since late May
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Oil prices surged Thursday as worries about attacks in the Red Sea exacerbated supply concerns in a petroleum market limited by the quasi-shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz. The post Brent oil prices rise above $100 first time since late May appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: 2027: I’ll run decent, issue-based campaign — Gombe PDP guber candidate
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The gubernatorial candidate of the ruling All Progressives Congress (APC) in Gombe State, Dr Jamil Isyaku Gwamna, has The post 2027: I’ll run decent, issue-based campaign — Gombe PDP guber candidate appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: 0sun guber, 2027 elections: Results management must be open, transparent – Yiaga Africa to INEC
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Yiaga Africa has urged the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, to establish a result transmission protocol that clarifies the journey of election results from polling units to their final destination. Yiaga Africa emphasized the necessity for the public to be informed about the handling

item.title: Golf: Nigeria Cup 2026 to honour tradition, raise championship standards
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Held annually to commemorate Nigeria's Independence celebrations, the Nigeria Cup has grown into one of the country's most established amateur golf tournaments The post Golf: Nigeria Cup 2026 to honour tradition, raise championship standards appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: 2027: Deregistration Of Political Parties Portends Danger, Says Falana
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: According to him, political exclusion has historically encouraged undemocratic forces to undermine democratic institutions across Africa. The post 2027: Deregistration Of Political Parties Portends Danger, Says Falana appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: One feared killed, two​ rescued in attack on Kwara community
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: One person was reportedly feared dead, while two victims were rescued in an attack by armed bandits on Aboki community in Kaiama Local Government Area of Kwara State. Residents of the community were thrown into fear and confusion on Tuesday as heavily armed unknown gunmen stormed the community in th

item.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email

item.title: Soldier Declared Wanted For Allegedly Supplying Uniforms To Terrorists
item.dc:creator: Information Nigeria
item.description: The Nigerian Army Ordnance Corps has declared one of its personnel, Mohammed Yusuf Amutu, wanted over his alleged involvement in the illegal sale and supply of military uniforms to terrorists and other criminals. Oluwatope Dorcas Aluko, the assistant spokesperson for the corps, said in a statement o

item.title: The rising cost of capital for companies today is starting to spook the stock market: ‘The worry is the spending might not pay off’
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Alphabet says it plans to throw even more money at the AI build-out. The Iran war, oil back at $100 a barrel and rising bond yields make it all the more difficult for the market to stomach.

item.title: 2027: Ex-Lagos guber candidate Salis meets Atiku
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Alliance for Democracy, AD, governorship candidate in Lagos State, Owolabi Salis, on Thursday had a closed-door meeting with the African Democratic Congress, ADC, presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar. Salis disclosed that the meeting was part of consultations with key political leaders and

item.title: Bandits kill 23 farmers in Zamfara
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: A fresh attack by suspected armed bandits has killed 23 people on their farmlands in Sauna District of The post Bandits kill 23 farmers in Zamfara appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: EFCC returns N108m recovered from former employees to Lagos company
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Economic and Financial Crimes Commission, EFCC, has handed over N108 million recovered during the investigation of an alleged fraud involving two former employees of Greenwich Planet Sun King Nigeria Limited to the company in Lagos. A statement by the EFCC said the money was presented in bank dr

item.title: Benue insecurity won’t be tolerated again, IGP Disu declares
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Describing Benue as a state once renowned for peace and hospitality, Disu lamented that it had been overtaken by violence fuelled by armed criminal herders, kidnappers, bandits, cultists and other criminal gangs, warning that the cycle of attacks and reprisals must stop. The post Benue insecurity wo

item.title: Tinubu okays expansion of Nigerian Army to 12 divisions, orders recruitment of 28,000 – Presidency
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has approved a major restructuring of the Nigerian Army, expanding its structure from eight to 12 divisions and authorising the recruitment of 28,000 additional personnel, the State House announced on Thursday. The post Tinubu okays expansion of Nigerian Army to 12 divisions, o

item.title: Tinubu Approves 4 New Army Divisions, 28,000 Soldiers
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has approved the expansion of the Nigerian Army from 8 to 12 divisions, along with the recruitment of 28,000 additional personnel. The new structure will create four additional divisions in Makurdi, Ilorin, Jalingo and Benin City, with implementation scheduled to be completed i

item.title: Spanish Club Plot Move For Maja
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Spanish outfit Valencia are weighing up a move for Nigerian striker Josh Maja following his departure from Sky Bet Championship club West Bromwich Albion, Completesports.com reports. Maja is currently available on a free transfer after his contract with the Baggies expired at the end of the 2025/26

item.title: Senate asks SGF, HOS to ensure MDAs honour lawmakers’ invitations
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The upper chamber warned that it would not hesitate to impose appropriate sanctions on agencies that continue to disregard committee invitations. The post Senate asks SGF, HOS to ensure MDAs honour lawmakers’ invitations appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: “Don’t let me die” — Ngozi Nwosu appeals for ₦30m for surgeries
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The appeal comes 13 years after the veteran actress received public support to fund treatment for kidney- and heart-related complications. The post “Don’t let me die” — Ngozi Nwosu appeals for ₦30m for surgeries appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Abuja land agent, Omokamo has no title documents to disputed properties – EFCC witness tells court
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A witness of the Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC), Ahmed Gbigbadua, has told the Federal High Court in Abuja that a famous land agent, Rebecca Omokamo, has no title documents to three properties she allegedly sold to unsuspecting buyers. The witness said that both Omokamo and Godwin-I

item.title: Iraola: I Want To Build A Formidable Squad At Liverpool
item.dc:creator: Completesports

Speaking during preseason preparations in the United States, Andoni Iraola stressed that long-term success stems from the pitch’s foundational elements r

item.title: These 16 states are offering tax holidays as back-to-school costs soar to over $800 per student
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: As inflation continues to squeeze household budgets, parents and students are on a mission to find back-to-school bargains.

item.title: Kano Anti-Graft Agency Uncovers Sale Of Free Mosquito Nets
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Kano State Public Complaints and Anti-Corruption Commission (PCACC) says it has uncovered the alleged diversion and sale of more than 1,000 insecticide-treated mosquito nets meant for free distribution to residents. The commission disclosed this in a statement on Thursday by its Public Relations

item.title: Nigerian Army Declares Soldier Wanted Over Alleged Uniform Supply To Terrorists
item.dc:creator: Leadership

item.title: Arise TV goofs, no burning of any motor parks in Osun, Police, press go round parks
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Shocked by the wrong report, the Osun State Police Command invited journalists on Wednesday morning to visit all the motor parks so the public would be availed of the truth to avoid unnecessary tension among residents of the state.

item.title: NSIB Probes Runway Excursion Involving Enugu Air Jet At Benin Airport
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Nigerian Safety Investigation Bureau, NSIB, has commenced an investigation into a runway excursion involving an Enugu Air Embraer E170 aircraft at Benin Airport on Thursday. The aircraft, operating as Flight 4264 with registration 5N-ENR, overran the end of Runway 05 while landing from Lagos at

item.title: The stock market’s calm surface is cracking. Here’s how to prepare for an August shock.
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: August is historically brutal for market volatility. Here’s how to protect your portfolio.

item.title: Amnesty demands release or trial of man held by DSS since 2023
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Amnesty International has called on the Department of State Services (DSS) to either immediately release or arraign Engr. Tahiru Mohammed Salihu in court, alleging that he has been held in custody since February 2023 without access to his family or legal representatives. In a statement, the human ri

item.title: NPFL: Kwara United Unveil 16 New Signings, Retain 14 As Title Chase Begins
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Kwara United Football Club on Wednesday in Ilorin unveiled no fewer than 16 new players ahead of the 2026/27 Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) season, with the primary objective of challenging for the league and Federation Cup titles, Completesports.com reports. The Harmony Boys also renewed th

item.description: Governor Peter Mbah of Enugu State has called for a fundamental overhaul of Nigeria's education system, urging schools to focus on producing problem-solvers and innovators. item.title: Easywin Nigeria concludes World Cup campaign
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The World Cup promotion was among several campaigns launched during the tournament to sustain fans' interest, even as Nigeria's Super Eagles missed out on qualification for the finals. The post Easywin Nigeria concludes World Cup campaign appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: NPFL: Kano Pillars sign new goalkeeper from Warri Wolves
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Kano Pillars have announced the signing of goalkeeper Efevwerha Joke, DAILY POST reports. Joke joined Sai Masu Gida from Warri Wolves. Kano Pillars confirmed the shot-stopper’s arrival on social med​ia on Thursday. Joke signed a two-year contract with the four-time Nigeria Premier Football Lea

item.title: World Bank estimates Venezuela earthquakes caused $19.6bn in damage
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Report says 47 percent of physical damage from pair of earthquakes impacted residential buildings.

item.title: PDP faction blames APC for crises in opposition parties, vows to be on ballot
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: “It's regrettable, indeed very sad and unfortunate, that when you begin to trace the genesis of those crises, they will take you back to the footsteps of the party in government.” The post PDP faction blames APC for crises in opposition parties, vows to be on ballot appeared first on Premium Times N

item.title: Air Peace suspends Benin flights, gives reason
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Air Peace has suspended all flights to and from the Benin Airport after the temporary closure of the airport’s runway following a runway incursion. In a statement on Thursday, the airline said that the suspension would affect all its scheduled operations to the airport. According to the airline, the

item.title: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing

item.description: The Series A was led by Battery Ventures, bringing AegisAI total funding to $49 million.

item.title: Dangote Suspends Petrol Sale In Dollar As Nigeria’s Enhances Imports
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: In a proactive gesture to minimize petroleum price shock in the domestic market, Dangote Petroleum Refinery has resumed the sale of Premium Motor Spirit (PMS) or petrol in naira, providing a measure of relief to marketers and consumers. The Gantry price is fixed at N1,215/ litre. Following a week

item.title: MC Fish Begs For Freedom Amid Marital Rift With Anita Joseph
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The estranged husband of Nollywood actress Anita Joseph, Fisayo Olagunju, better known as MC Fish has thrown shades at an unidentified person. MC Fish, in a post on his Instagram story, narrated how the unidentified person is calling God’s name, yet doing evil behind closed doors. According to him,

item.title: Police arrest four suspected kidnappers, rescue abducted woman
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The police said all four suspects confessed to their individual roles in the crime. The post Police arrest four suspected kidnappers, rescue abducted woman appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: ‎Theatre Icon Mama Iya Osogbo To Be Celebrated At 96 With Biography
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ‎Veteran Nollywood and theatre matriarch Mama Grace Owoola Oyin-Adejobi, popularly known as Iya Osogbo, will be honoured on Sunday, 13th September 2026, as she marks 96 years of life, faith and service to Nigerian culture. ‎This was made known by Sola Oyin Adejobi, who noted that the celebration wil

item.title: PDP will survive leadership crisis, says Turaki
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Chairman of the PDP Interim National Working Committee (INWC), Tanimu Turaki, has said the opposition party will not die despite its current travails. The post PDP will survive leadership crisis, says Turaki appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Turkish Court Orders Compensation For 33 People Burnt Alive
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The compensation, the amount of which will remain unknown until the publication of the reasoned judgement, "cannot constitute full reparation for these families", Kursun stressed. item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Ali al-Zaidi will hold talks with senior Iranian officials on cooperation, shared interests and security.

item.title: My second husband and I have kids from previous relationships. Should my house go to him or my children if I die first?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “We live in a house that I purchased entirely with money I had before we were married.”

item.title: Anambra threatens to arrest, prosecute illegal traditional rulers

item.description: The Anambra State Government has threatened to arrest and prosecute anyone parading himself as the traditional ruler of any community without recognition and certification. item.dc:creator: Premium Times

item.title: Stakeholders Back Senate On Physical Offices For Social Media Platforms
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Legislative move by the Senate to compel the establishment of physical offices by social media platforms in Nigeria got significant acceleration on Thursday with support by most of the stakeholders during public hearing. The bill was sponsored by Senator Ned Nwoko. The bill, sponsored by Senator Ned

item.title: Trump stuns Saudis by pinning nuclear deal to Israel ties
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed. Riyadh has long resisted joining the so-called Abraham Accords with Israel, insisting especially after the

item.title: Zamfara Government Lauds IOM For Empowering Over 90 Women With Business Start-Up Kits
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Zamfara State Government has commended the International Organization for Migration (IOM) for empowering more than 90 women in Gusau and Bungudu Local Government Areas with business start-up kits aimed at improving family livelihoods, promoting self-reliance, psychosocial wellbeing and st

item.title: Court Refuses To Review Miyetti Allah Leader Bodejo’s ₦2bn Bail
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: A Federal High Court sitting in Maitama, Abuja, on Thursday declined to rule on an application by the leader of Miyetti Allah Kautal Hore, Bello Bodejo, seeking a review of his stringent bail conditions in his ongoing money laundering trial. Justice Inyang Edem Ekwo said he could not determine the a

item.title: Nigerian students win laurels at 2026 African Mathematics Olympiad
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nigeria has recorded another feat in international academic competition as its representatives won multiple medals at the 2026 Pan African Mathematics Olympiad (PAMO) held in Yamoussoukro, Côte d’Ivoire. The Nigerian team secured five silver medals and two bronze medals across the Pan African

item.title: Miami to Sell Griffin a Historic Site Next to New Citadel HQ
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Miami agreed to sell Ken Griffin a site across the street from the planned Citadel headquarters in the Brickell financial district that currently houses an historic cottage, completing the billionaire’s goal of owning the entire square block.

item.title: Reinstating Fedorov 'only right decision Zelensky can make', MP says
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Ousted Ukrainian defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov said on Thursday he would ​accept no other government position besides his former job, escalating a political crisis that has gripped Ukraine at a pivotal point in the war with Russia. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Ukranian MP Liudmyla Buimis

item.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a memo to employees, Patreon CEO Jack Conte writes that the company isn't making these changes "because we believe AI replaces humans," but says AI has "fundamentally transformed the tech in

Th

item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) said President Bola Tinubu is celebrating President Donald Trump’s letter to him as if it were a referendum on the state of the nation. The party however stated that there is noting strange in Donald Trump’s letter, noting that diplomatic corresponde

item.title: Tinubu approves expansion, recruitment of 28,000 personnel into Nigerian Army
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has approved the expansion of the Nigerian Army’s structure from eight to twelve divisions. The governor also directed the Nigeria Police Force and other securi

item.title: Collapse after a landslide: Starmer’s fall may not be the last, By Azu Ishiekwene
item.dc:creator: Premium Times Opinion
item.description: With a drastic decline in public trust of politicians and public institutions, it’s not surprising that Starmer’s landslide fizzled before he could fully milk it. The palace coup that forced out the Prime Minister was not because Labour MPs loved him less, but because they love themselves more. Whee

item.title: Banking stocks lift NGX by 0.98% as market cap gains N1.58trn
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Nigerian equities market rebounded strongly on Thursday as renewed buying interest in banking heavyweights and other blue-chip stocks... The post Banking stocks lift NGX by 0.98% as market cap gains N1.58trn appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Deadly Wildfires In Europe Force Thousands To Flee
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Tourists and locals have told of desperate escapes from flames. The post Deadly Wildfires In Europe Force Thousands To Flee appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Senate Passes Bills On FCT Judiciary, Waterways, Nicotine Products
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Senate on Thursday approved three bills transmitted by the House of Representatives, paving the way for the expansion of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) High Court, the reform of the National Inland Waterways Administration (NIWA), and stricter regulation of tobacco and nicotine products. Th

item.title: Kano gov pledges increased investment in education, youth empowerment
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Kano State Governor Abba Kabir Yusuf has reaffirmed his administration’s commitment to prioritising human capital development through sustained investment in education, skills acquisition, youth empowerment, and job creation. The governor made the pledge on Wednesday while inaugurating the Int

item.title: Bordeaux wildfire burns 3,100 hectares, 20,000 evacuated
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: A wildfire near Bordeaux, France, has burned 3,100 hectares (7,660 acres) of forest

item.title: Kogi govt begins compensation for Okene demolition victims
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Kogi State Government has commenced the payment of compensation to victims affected by the demolition of structures at Idojie... The post Kogi govt begins compensation for Okene demolition victims appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Why Women Shouldn’t Get Married In Their 20s – Toke Makinwa
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Nigerian media personality and Nollywood actress Toke Makinwa has shared her thoughts about women getting married in their 20s. According to the 41-year-old mother of one, women have no business getting married at that age. She noted that most women are still discovering themselves in their 20s and

item.title: Lagos Assembly Approves N200bn Bond, FAAC Funding Plan
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Lagos State House of Assembly has approved the state government’s request to participate in the Federation Account Allocation Committee (FAAC) Receivables Discounting Programme. The House also approved the proposed issuance of a ₦200 billion bond under the state’s existing ₦1 trillion Debt and H

item.title: Police Recover Stolen Vehicle, Arrest Two Suspects With Toy Guns, ATM Cards In Niger State
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The suspects remain in police custody as investigations continue. The post Police Recover Stolen Vehicle, Arrest Two Suspects With Toy Guns, ATM Cards In Niger State appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Ngozi Nwosu Seeks Financial Support For Life-Saving Surgery
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Veteran Nollywood actress Ngozi Nwosu on Thursday appealed to the general public for financial assistance amid a battle with an undisclosed illness. The actress, who made the plea in a video shared on her Instagram page, was seen lying on a hospital bed. She stated that she urgently needs N30 millio

item.title: Atiku’s Desire To Become President Has Closed His Eyes To Virtues Of Statesmanship, Presidency Says
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Presidency has said that the former Vice President Atiku Abubakar’s desire to become the nation’s president has closed his eyes to the virtues of statesmanship, decorum and propriety expected of a leader. It also accused the opposition figure of presiding over a privatization heist w

item.title: Nobody can kill PDP, we’ll be on 2027 ballot — Turaki
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Luminous Jannamike ABUJA — The Peoples Democratic Party, PDP, on Thursday declared that it would be on the ballot in all the 2027 general elections, insisting that no individual or group could destroy the party despite its current leadership crisis. The declaration came as the party’s Inter

item.title: FRSC records zero road crashes in Sokoto, Kebbi, Zamfara during special operation
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC), Zone RS10, has announced that no road traffic crashes were recorded across Sokoto, Kebbi, and Zamfara states during its three-day special enforcement operation, codenamed “Operation Daidaita Lodi” (Operation Load Limit). The exercise, conducted from

item.title: BREAKING: Reps Pass Executive Bill On State Police
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: On Thursday, the members of the House of Representatives passed the Executive Bill seeking to establish state police, marking a significant step toward decentralising Nigeria’s policing system. The bill was passed after the House considered and adopted the report of the Committee on the Review of th

item.title: IGP Announces 14-Day Clearance Operation To Flush Out Bandits In Benue
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The IGP also urged young people not to allow themselves to be recruited by influential individuals to perpetrate violence, particularly during elections. The post IGP Announces 14-Day Clearance Operation To Flush Out Bandits In Benue appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: LPPC Elevates 68 Lawyers To Senior Advocate Rank
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The Legal Practitioners’ Privileges Committee (LPPC) has approved the elevation of 68 legal practitioners to the prestigious rank of Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN), the highest professional distinction for advocates in the country. The decision was reached at the committee’s 174th plen
